table_of_content.title: Introduction
text: Aurora is a trading company founded in 2021. The company is engaged in parallel imports into Russia.  Its main activity consists in individual search of goods around the world in accordance with the requests of clients. That is, the company imports any goods, from clothing to car parts. The main goal of the company is to meet customers' needs for high quality products and fast delivery. The company did not have its own product, as it acted as a dealer in the Russian market. The company wants to make serious changes in its activities, so it has made a request to develop a strategy for buying cars in UAE and then importing them into Russia. Solving this case is a very hot topic at the moment, because of the world situation the automotive market is very depressed.

The automotive industry in Russia is the most important sector for the economic growth and development of any country. This industry is growing and developing rapidly in different parts of the world, and Russia is one of the largest markets for automotive products. However, due to recent supply chain disruptions and component shortages, many companies in the automotive market are facing problems. For example, many models of cars are in short supply or not available at all in car dealerships, and problems have arisen with the guarantee on cars due to the withdrawal of official dealers.

The UAE is one of the largest automotive markets in the Gulf region. The UAE automotive market is represented by many global manufacturers such as Toyota, Nissan, Ford, Chevrolet, BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Audi, and many others. Most of the cars are sold under the "import-sales" scheme, which means that they have been brought into the country from other countries of the world, mainly from Europe, USA and Japan. The condition of cars in the UAE is considered good because in most cases they are in excellent condition and have been thoroughly inspected before being sold. This is because there are strict rules and regulations governing the technical condition of cars in the UAE, and a thorough inspection is carried out on site before a permit for sale is issued. Car prices in the UAE are lower than in other countries due to the lack of taxes on cars and more affordable fuel prices. Another important element of the UAE car market is car auctions. There are several major car auctions in the UAE where you can buy used cars at a lower price than in the regular market. At car auctions you can find a wide range of cars of different brands and models as well as different classes and categories. The purpose of this consulting project is to develop a Automobile export strategy for Aurora. Relevance of the work: The relevance of this project due to the current situation in the automobile market in Russia, which was severely affected by failures in the supply chain and the shortage of components. This has led to a shortage of both new and used vehicles, creating a potential opportunity for new market entrants. Therefore, this paper will develop a strategy for Aurora, a Kyrgyz company, to purchase cars in UAE and then import them into Russia. During the meeting with the company representatives the following aspects were discussed: what cars are the easiest to buy in UAE? How to pay for the cars? How to transport the cars and how to bring them to Russia? What about taxes?  All of these aspects will be reflected in this project.
Objective of the research: The main objective of this consulting project is to develop a strategy for the purchase of cars in UAE by the Kyrgyz company Aurora with subsequent importation into Russia.
To achieve the goal of this work, I will solve the following tasks:
Evaluate Aurora's resources and competencies
Analyze the Russian automotive market
Analyze the car market in the UAE
To conduct a comparative analysis of prices for cars in Russia and the UAE
Analyze the segment of potential buyers in Russia
Analyze the competitors in the UAE car market
Analyze possible market strategy
Formation of prices for cars taking into account the competitive environment
Examine customs rules and procedures for importing cars from UAE to Kyrgyzstan
Examine customs rules and procedures for importing cars from Kyrgyzstan to Russia
Make a logistical scheme for delivering cars to Russia
Make a financial planning
Analyze the risks and prepare recommendations
Subject of the study: development of a strategy for the purchase of cars in the UAE by the Kyrgyz company "Aurora" with subsequent importation into Russia.

Object of the study: the market of cars in the UAE, the Russian market, customs regulations and procedures for importing cars from Kyrgyzstan to Russia, the segment of potential buyers, the competitive environment, the logistics scheme of car delivery, car prices and risk analysis.

Structure of the work: the thesis consists of an introduction, three chapters, a conclusion, a reference and an appendix. The first chapter is about Aurora's analysis and clarification of the managerial problem. The second chapter is devoted to the analysis of the UAE and Russian market, as well as aspects of potential customers and competitors. The third chapter is to develop a strategy for the purchase of cars in the UAE market of the Kyrgyz company Aurora with the subsequent importation of cars into Russia.
Research methodology for developing a strategy for the purchase of cars in the UAE by a Kyrgyz company with subsequent importation into Russia:

Research Design: The research design will be exploratory and qualitative, aimed at identifying the key factors for the development of a successful strategy for the purchase of cars in the UAE by a Kyrgyz company with subsequent importation into Russia. The study will include a review of existing literature on the Russian automobile market and the UAE market, as well as a review of literature related to logistics and customs activities.

Data collection: The data collection process will involve primary and secondary data sources. Primary data sources will include interviews with Kyrgyz company representatives and experts in the Russian automobile market and the UAE market. Secondary data sources will include industry reports, academic journals and online databases.

Internet research: various Internet sources will be used to obtain data on the UAE car market and the Russian car market, to compare prices and delivery conditions for cars, to make logistical charts, to study customs import regulations, and to obtain information on potential competitors and buyers.
Questionnaire survey: will be used to collect data on the current situation in the Russian automotive market, preferences and needs of potential buyers to determine the target audience.

PESTLE analysis: PESTLE analysis will be conducted to assess political, economic, socio-cultural, technological, legal and environmental factors that may affect the importation of cars into Russia from Kyrgyzstan.
Data analysis: various data analysis tools, such as Excel, will be used to analyze data from different sources more accurately and comprehensively.

Competitive profile matrix will be used to compare our company with our competitors. This tool will help assess Aurora's competitiveness in the automotive industry market by comparing its products, quality, prices and other characteristics with its competitors.

Market Entry Strategy: Based on the results of PESTLE and other analyses, a strategy will be developed for the purchase of cars in the UAE by a Kyrgyz company with subsequent importation into Russia. The strategy will include recommendations on product range, logistics schemes, customs aspects, pricing. The market entry strategy will also take into account the resources and capabilities of the company and risks.

Conclusion: The results of the study and recommendations will be summarized in the format of a report with a finalized strategy for the purchase of cars in the UAE by a Kyrgyz company with subsequent importation into Russia. Thus, the conclusion of the project will be based on the generalization of the results of the analysis of the Russian car market and the market of cars in the UAE, as well as a comparative analysis of prices in Russia and the UAE. Also on the results of the analysis of the segment of potential buyers  and competitors. In addition, the conditions of purchase and delivery of cars in Kyrgyzstan, customs regulations and procedures for importing cars from Kyrgyzstan to Russia, and the logistics scheme for delivering cars to Russia will be taken into account.

Competitive profile matrix and PESTEL will help to assess the competitive environment and identify advantages and disadvantages of competitors. Risk analysis and recommendations on pricing for cars will also be carried out, considering the competitive environment.

The use of the Delphi method will allow to get expert opinion from different stakeholders and take into account their views in the formation of recommendations for the strategy of purchasing cars in the UAE by the Kyrgyz company "Aurora" and then import them into Russia.

sub_elements.title: 1.2 Managerial problem statement
text: The managerial problem of Aurora is the company's desire to work in the market of cars from Russia, exporting cars from the UAE. Aurora was established as a trading company that imports products to Russia under the customer's order. However, due to the lack of orientation in a certain direction, it is difficult for the company to manage and distribute its resources competently and efficiently. As mentioned above, the company is engaged in the import of absolutely different goods to Russia from auto parts to clothing.

After an interview with representatives of the company, the following task was set, to develop an export strategy for the supply of cars from the UAE to Russia. Due to some events around the world many car dealers left Russia and the car market is in a critical condition. There is a shortage of imported cars in the Russian market. Representatives of the company chose the UAE market for the following reasons: firstly, the company has a representative in the UAE, which would help to resolve any issues related to the transportation of cars from UAE to Kyrgyzstan in a more expeditious and efficient manner; secondly, the company considers this market more suitable for the transportation of cars because before they come to Russia they need to be cleared in Kyrgyzstan; thirdly, in their opinion prices of cars in UAE differ several times from the prices of cars in Russia.

However, they are not sure about the prospects of working with export cars. That is why it is necessary to study the Russian car market and the UAE car market. It is important to find out how much the cars sold in the UAE are similar to the demands of car buyers from Russia. It is important to study the prices of cars in the UAE and Russia in order to calculate the benefits of working in this direction. Also, to develop in this direction it is necessary to study potential customers, so you can build an effective marketing strategy. As well as to study competitors in order to identify the advantages of the company, as well as the factors on which the company needs to work to improve its competitiveness. In order to understand the profitability of working with export cars from the UAE, it is necessary to calculate the associated costs of logistics and customs. Since the price difference for cars from UAE to Russia may not be so significant after calculating these points. Thus, the work should begin with an analysis of the automotive market in Russia.

sub_elements.title: 2.1 Russian Automotive Market Analysis
text: To begin with, we should consider the concept of the automobile market itself. The automobile market is commonly understood as a set of economic relations that promote interaction between market participants for the exchange of automobile equipment for cash or cash equivalents. In addition, the automobile market is a territorially bounded space used by individual entrepreneurs or individuals for the sale of vehicles and their spare parts. There are four main segments of the Russian automotive market: passenger cars and light commercial vehicles, trucks, buses and the segment of special purpose vehicles. This project will consider the first segment: passenger cars and light commercial vehicles.

To begin with, let's look at the size of the automotive market over the past few years, relying on the following tool: sales statistics from the AEB (Association of European Businesses), which is compiled from data on shipments to dealers provided by the brands themselves and the statistics published by the Federal Customs Service of the Russian Federation.
The passenger car segment is the most mass market for cars in Russia. The dynamics of passenger car sales in Russia in recent years have been as follows:

Political factors
1. Taxes and import laws

In 2022, it became impossible to supply cars to Russia from unfriendly countries. In response to the sanctions restrictions, Russia developed a procedure for parallel imports. In December 2022, the European Commission published a list of measures against violators of the sanctions regime against Russia. The document provides for liability, including for the supply of sanctioned goods to Russia, up to criminal liability: up to five years of imprisonment. What is at stake here is primarily investment in the Russian economy. In Russia, on the contrary, a number of restrictions have been abolished in response to these measures. Thus, on the contrary, the criminal responsibility for the unauthorized importation of goods by the right holder was abolished in the summer, which opened the possibility for the so-called parallel imports. The introduction of sanctions first turned out to be to the advantage of car sellers in Russia. In the spring of 2022, consumers reacted to the alarming news by hastily withdrawing money from deposits and spending it on something tangible, particularly cars. With panic demand and the cessation of deliveries through official channels, a "seller's market" emerged, where scarce cars began to be sold at incredible markups. Almost immediately there was a demand for alternative supplies of cars.

The main document in the import of cars is the RF Government Decree of May 12, 2022 № 855 "On approval of the rules of mandatory requirements for certain wheeled vehicles and their conformity assessment" (as amended and supplemented), which approves the rules for the application of mandatory requirements in respect of cars. Decree of the Russian Federation of May 12, 2022 (as amended) takes into account

Technical regulation of the Customs Union «About safety of wheeled vehicles» It is allowed to release cars without ERA-GLONASS system until August 1, 2022, with compulsory retrofitting till February 1, 2023. As for the braking system, it is allowed to release cars without ABS system.

Minimum emission requirements have been reduced from Euro-5 to Euro-0 (except for some trucks with a minimum of Euro-1). In terms of passive safety, co-frontal and side crash tests have been eliminated. Mandatory automatic triggering of ERA-GLONASS system in case of airbags deployment was excluded - now it is also possible to activate the system in manual mode. Due to the lack of supply of components for the period until February 1, 2023, special rules for the application of mandatory requirements for individual wheeled vehicles and assessment of their compliance have been established.
The relaxations apply to the largest manufacturers, the list of which will be determined by the Ministry of Industry and Trade.

The amount of duty depends directly on the value of the car, the engine displacement of the car and the age of the car: for cars not more than 3 years old: up to 8,500 euro equivalent 54% of the value, but not less than 2,5 Euro per 1 cc of the engine capacity; with the value over 8500 Euro but not more than 16700 Euro in the equivalent of 48% of the value, but not less than 3,5 Euro per 1 cc of the engine capacity; with the value over 16700 Euro but not more than 42300 Euro in the equivalent of 48% of the value, but not less than 5,5 Euro per 1 cc of the engine capacity; with the value over 16300 Euro in the equivalent of 35% of the value, but not less than 5,5 Euro per 1 cc of the engine capacity. cc of engine displacement; worth over €4,2300 but no more than €8,4500 in the equivalent of 48% of the cost but no less than €7.5 per cc of engine displacement; worth over €8,4500 but no more than €169,000 in the equivalent of 48% of the cost but no less than €15 per cc of engine displacement; worth over €16,000 in the equivalent of 48% of the cost but no less than €20 per cc of engine displacement. The salvage fee is paid only once at the importation of a vehicle. It is intended for the disposal of a vehicle after it has been taken out of service. For individuals the amount of the scrapping fee depends on the age of the car. If the car is younger than 3 years - 3400 rubles, otherwise - 5200 rubles. Ministry of Industry and Trade of Russia proposed to ban import of cars from friendly countries by simplified requirements from June 1, according to the website of the Ministry.
2. Subsidies for the production of hybrid vehicles

In 2020, the Russian Federation approved a program for the development of the automobile industry, under which the production of more environmentally friendly vehicles is stimulated.  Under this program, it is possible to receive subsidies for the development of production and equipment. It also provides for lower tax rates and tax incentives. And measures are taken to increase the demand for environmentally friendly cars.

Also on September 1, 2022, the government signed a decree: "On Approval of the Rules for granting subsidies from the federal budget to Russian organizations to provide state support for the creation of a testing base for certification and development work in the design of cars with low carbon footprint".  Under this decree the government offers subsidies for the following items: staff costs, material costs, overhead costs and so on.
State program of preferential auto lending

The purpose of this program is to support domestic car manufacturers. Under this program, potential buyers are given a discount when buying a domestic car on credit. This program applies to medical workers, educators and mobilized, volunteers and contract servicemen participating in the SSO, as well as members of their families. Under the terms of the program the basic discount on the payment of the down payment on the loan is 20% of the value of the car. The discount on Russian-made electric cars for all categories of borrowers is 25%.
Economic Factors
1. Exchange rate and inflation

At the end of 2022 the dollar exchange rate exceeded 70 rubles for the first time in a long time. Such fluctuations may affect the automotive industry in the following way: car prices will rise. Prices are in direct correlation with inflation, the ruble exchange rate, demand, and the availability of cars. With an increase in the exchange rate, there is an increase in prices, including for cars. In the first place, prices have changed for cars of foreign companies.  That is, the fall in the ruble has less of an effect on the cost of domestic cars. Usually car companies have increased prices by 3-4% every year because of inflation. However, at this point in time, the percentage has almost doubled to 7%.
2. Oil prices

North Sea Brent crude oil prices in March 2023 are between $81 and $86 a barrel. On March 10, at the close of trading the price of oil on the London Stock Exchange fell by 2.38% compared to March 1 to $82.76 per barrel. The discount of the Russian Urals oil grade to the benchmark Brent in 2022 increased from $20 to $30 per barrel. The average price of Urals in January-February 2023 was $49.52 per barrel, compared with $88.89 per barrel in the first two months of the previous year. The average price of Urals was $49.56 per barrel in February, which is 1.86 times less than the average price a year ago ($92.15 per barrel). Russian oil is affected by the embargo on oil and oil products, the price ceiling on Russian oil, plans to increase the mineral extraction tax for oil companies and a 5% decrease in oil production in March compared to January levels. The export duty on oil also went up as of March 1.  Low oil prices in Russia have led to a drop in demand for cars due to the decreased purchasing power of the population and the devaluation of the ruble. Car sales in Russia almost mirror the dynamics of oil prices.
3. Prices for materials and resources

Dealers reported that the volume of cars has decreased significantly, which can be seen in the models presented in the showroom itself. Since many brands have left the Russian market, their representatives cannot provide the buyer with a guarantee and quick delivery of both the car to order and the necessary spare parts for it. The prices of parts for foreign cars have increased several times because of the sanctions. Dealers cannot provide a warranty on some cars due to the lack of spare parts. However, for the assembly of domestic cars, foreign-made components are also used, and their prices may change.  This may affect the final cost of cars for consumers.
4. Buying power

The purchase of new cars fell by 40% in 2023. At the beginning of 2022, 86 thousand cars were sold, and at the beginning of 2023 it is 2 times less, namely 45 thousand cars. People have begun to buy fewer foreign cars, this is due to the withdrawal of foreign companies from the Russian market, and as a consequence the growth of prices for these cars. However, Russians have become more active in buying Russian and Chinese brands.
5. Commercial use of vehicles

In 2022, there is a decline in this market by 58.5%. In 2023, the market of passenger cars and passenger commercial vehicles may grow by 12%.  The development of the market can be influenced by the appearance of new cars. "We expect that at least 5-7 more new brands will appear in 2023, or even more. Therefore, the market will develop one way or another. The capacity of the market exists, the need for cars exists," said the head of the AEB Automobile Manufacturers Committee. For commercial activities, for example, in cabs, cars of Chinese manufacturers such as Cherry have become actively used.
6. Foreign fuel companies' withdrawal

Gasoline prices in Russia remain stable. They have not been affected by the departure of Western companies from the Russian market. On the domestic market, the government uses a damper as a tool to protect against price changes. The state sets certain benchmarks for gasoline and diesel fuel prices on the Russian market. The damping mechanism provides for payments to oil companies for shortfall in income when fuel is sold on the domestic market at prices below world prices.
Social factors

The environmental agenda in Russia has recently come to the fore more and more often. Industrial waste emissions are worsening the state of the atmosphere. The combustion of automobile fuel is bad for the air. Harmful particles pollute the ozone layer and destroy it. Once in the atmosphere, they cause acid rain, which in turn pollutes the earth and water bodies. All of these factors cause cancer, cardiovascular disease, and animal extinctions. Air pollution also contributes to climate change, global warming and an increase in ultraviolet radiation from the sun. A way to make a statement about global warming is to buy an electric car, and they are more economical to maintain. At the moment, Europe and America are focused on environmentally friendly cars, so now in Russia electric cars are seen as modern and innovative cars, which are not only interested in those who are afraid for the future of the planet.

In Russia, the issue of increasing the demographic fund is acute. The policy, which includes benefits and state support, of the country is aimed at raising the demographics. As of 2021, one-third of families have two or more children. Such families, of course, choose family cars, which are more comfortable than conventional sedans: more roomy, free in the cabin.
Legal factors

When buying cars, consumers should pay attention to the following points: the seller is obliged in a timely manner in a clear and accessible form to bring to the buyer's attention the necessary and reliable information about the cars.

Including the company name (name) and location (legal address) of the goods manufacturer, location of the organization (organizations), authorized by the manufacturer (seller) to accept claims from buyers and repair and maintenance of goods.

If the goods purchased by the buyer were used, or a defect (deficiencies) were eliminated in it, the buyer must be provided with information about it. The seller must warn the buyer about the defects of the goods not only verbally, but also in writing (on the label of goods, sales receipt or in any other way). When selling cars, the seller is obliged to acquaint the consumer at his request with one of the following documents: certificate or declaration of conformity; a copy of the certificate, certified by the original holder of the certificate, a notary or the body for the certification of goods, which issued the certificate; shipping documents issued by the manufacturer or supplier (seller) and containing for each item of goods information about confirming its compliance with established requirements (certificate number, duration, the authority that issued the certificate or the registration number of the declaration of conformity, its duration, the name of the manufacturer or supplier (seller) that accepted the certificate of conformity)

These documents must be signed and sealed by the manufacturer (supplier, seller) with his address and telephone number. The vehicle warranty is the manufacturer's obligation to repair the vehicle within a specified period of time. The warranty is not provided by an authorized dealer, but by the manufacturer. Therefore, the dealer cannot officially remove the vehicle from the warranty. He can only refuse warranty repair in a specific case. For this purpose, the dealer checks the quality of the car. The car warranty is given not only by the manufacturer, but also by the law. If there is no warranty period, the buyer has the right to make a claim within two years of purchase.

Thus, we can conclude that the factors are largely aimed at the development of the domestic automobile industry. Domestic manufacturers provide a warranty on cars, unlike the remaining foreign manufacturers. There is a shortage of cars on the Russian official market, which has led to an increase in car prices. Restrictions on the supply of cars have led to the development of parallel imports. This affects the official car market in Russia, as cars are sold to customers in Russia without the involvement of official dealers and suppliers. It may also lead to inflated prices due to the limited supply from official dealers. As for the subsidies aimed at supporting the automobile industry in the Russian Federation, they can lead to an increase in the number of hybrid domestic cars. And subsidies for their purchase, can stimulate the demand for the purchase of such cars. Orientation of car manufacturers, dealers and consumers on the long-term perspective is practically impossible in these conditions. The geopolitical situation, exchange rate fluctuations and changes in oil prices may lead to volatility in the automotive industry in Russia.
Turning to the most popular car brands, the statistics according to autostat looks like this:

The most popular car brands on the Russian market Are Lada, Hyundai, Renault, Cherry, Haval, Toyota, Geely, Skoda, Volkswagen, Nissan and Mitsubishi. Lada is still the leader in sales of its cars on the Russian market. Foreign car brands were more prevalent in the Russian car market in 2021, but due to the departure of dealers, they declined in 2022. However, according to autostat, a total of 1,137,227 cars were sold in 2021 and 446,314 cars were sold in 2022. Sales have dropped almost threefold. Many automakers have left Russia because of the current world situation. The Russian automobile market is now dominated by Russian and Chinese automakers. However, they cannot fully satisfy the demand of Russians. For example, sales of the Lada brand in 2022 fell by 46% compared to 2021. Considering cars by class, we can conclude that the most popular are Sedans, Crossovers, Hatchbacks, SUVs, Minivans.

The following trends can be seen in passenger car segments: the most popular segment in Russia are cars with front-axle drive and engine capacity up to 1.6 liters. In 2020, this segment accounted for 57% of total passenger car sales. the crossover and SUV segment in Russia is also growing. In 2020, crossovers and SUVs accounted for 36% of total passenger car sales. The sedan and hatchback segment in Russia is shrinking. In 2020, sedans and hatchbacks accounted for 7% of total passenger car sales. Last year, Russia recorded a 7.5% drop in new car sales, indicating some reduction in demand for new cars in Russia.

Thus, Russia is currently experiencing a shortage of cars on the market, which leads to an increase in demand and higher prices.  This is due to several factors, including the reduction of car production in Russia due to the world situation, the lack of components for repairing or assembling cars.  This has happened because many dealers have left Russia and many car manufacturing plants have left the market. Domestic cars are now leading the market in sales, but most people prefer foreign brands, although it is almost impossible to buy them at the moment. It follows that people will be interested in the possibility of buying foreign-made cars.

sub_elements.title: 2.2 UAE Automotive Market Analysis
text: In 2020, the UAE used car market was valued at USD 9,590.41 million. By 2026, the market is expected to be valued at USD 20,630.41 million.  The UAE used car market is segmented based on vehicle type, fuel type, end-use, regional distribution, and competitive environment. Approximately 200,000 cars were sold in the UAE in 2021, a 26 percent increase over 2020. The total automotive market in the UAE grew 9.3 percent in the first half of 2022 compared to the same period in 2021. Total units sold in June were up 8.1 percent year-over-year. According to a report by Market Research UAE, the UAE auto market shows great promise and is seeing a recovery from the pandemic in 2020.   Revenue from passenger car sales is projected to reach $5.8 billion in 2023 after demand fully recovers. The UAE delivered more than 21,200,000  vehicles in the first two quarters of 2022, double the number delivered during the same period in 2021.

Toyota, continues to dominate the SUV and passenger car market. SUVs continue to dominate the UAE market, making up 50.5 percent of sales for the first half of the year. According to Vincent Weinen, senior managing director of Al-Futtaim Automotive, - "Toyota is the market leader in the SUV segment, accounting for 25.2 percent of sales from January to June. Toyota's total sales were up 11.4% year-over-year and market share was up 0.5%. Growth also continued among Al-Futtaim Automotive brands."

sub_elements.title: 2.3 Comparative analysis of automobile prices in the Russia and UAE
text: Dynamics of price changes for new cars in Russia. In 2021, the average price of a new passenger car in Russia was 1,600,000 rubles. This is about 10% higher than in 2020, when the average price was 1,400,000 rubles.

Thus, we can conclude that the average price of a new car in Russia has been increasing in recent years, but with slight fluctuations. This is due to both economic factors and changes in supply and demand in the car market. It should be noted that price dynamics for individual brands and models of cars can vary significantly. For example, due to the introduction of new emission standards, the most environmentally friendly cars may become more expensive, while cars with "heavier" engines may become cheaper. In addition, the price of cars depends on the demand for a particular brand or model, as well as the availability of counterparts and competitors on the market.

To better understand the differences between the car market in Russia and the UAE, we analyzed car prices. As in most countries, car prices in Russia vary greatly depending on the make, model and configuration. At the moment, the most affordable cars on the Russian market cost from 500,000 rubles. The average market price for a new car in Russia is between 1.2 and 2 million rubles. However, for some models of famous brands you may have to pay from 4-6 million rubles.
In the UAE, on average, the prices for new cars start at around 60,000 AED ($16,300) for a budget car, and may reach over 1 million AED ($272,000) for luxury models.

The sales tax on cars in the UAE is only 5%, which makes the cost of cars in this country more affordable than in Russia. In Russia, car prices depend not only on taxes, but also on customs duties, transportation costs, property taxes, security charges and other expenses.

text: Key success factors for companies in this industry
Brand reputation

Brand reputation is a key factor that affects customer trust. A company with a good reputation has a better chance of attracting and retaining customers. Customers often prefer to work with well-known and respected brands, because it is associated with the expected quality of goods and services.Here to a large extent it is not brand awareness that plays a role, but customer feedback
Logistics
Optimizing logistics processes and selecting a reliable delivery partner can help reduce costs and ensure fast delivery of goods.
Market knowledge
Knowledge of the specific market in which the company sells imported goods will help the company to identify the most popular products and effectively manage them.
Unique product offerings
By offering a unique product, a company can ensure its success. For example, exclusive brands or x product features. Uniqueness attracts consumers and provides a competitive advantage.
Working without intermediaries
This facilitates more transparent and efficient communication. It eliminates possible distortions or misunderstandings that can arise when transmitting information through intermediaries.
Marketing and promotion
Adapting marketing materials, considering local consumer preferences, and using appropriate sales and advertising channels can help develop an effective marketing strategy.

sub_elements.title: 3.2 Required documents when exporting a car from UAE to Kyrgyzstan
text: After receiving an order for a car, employees of the company begin to select the car. The characteristics of Aurora company states that the company has its own representative in the UAE, namely in Dubai. This representative begin to search for a car according to the characteristics of the customer in car markets, such as Al aweer car market. Employees are also starting searches on such inert sites as Copart Inc, Dubizzle Motors LLC, Al-Futtaim Groups

Next, the customer needs to pay for the services of Aurora, which include the procedure of selecting a car, its transportation from point A to point B. After the company Aurora has received payment for the car, there is a payment for this car in the UAE.
After the purchase of a car from the UAE, you need to collect the following documents for its transportation to Kyrgyzstan.

When taking the car from the UAE to Kyrgyzstan you will need to collect the following documents: passport of the car, technical passport, contract of sale, insurance policy, customs clearance documents.
Car Passport

This is an official document that certifies ownership and contains information about the car. Information that must be included in this passport: make, model, year of manufacture, vin number and so on.
Technical passport
This document contains information on technical characteristics of the vehicle, such as weight, dimensions, engine, etc.
Contract of Sale
This document must specify the date of purchase, value.
Car Insurance Policy

This is a necessary document that insures your car against possible damage during its transportation. The insurance policy must be valid for the entire period of transportation. As mentioned above, the cost of Aurora directly depends on the value of this insurance policy.
Customs clearance documents
This is a declaration of goods, customs invoice. It is important to consider the rules of each country through which the cargo is transported.
All of this set of documents is required in order to transport a car from the UAE through the countries to Kyrgyzstan.

sub_elements.title: 3.3 Required documents for importing and exporting cars from Kyrgyzstan
text: Customs clearance of a vehicle from the UAE upon arrival in Kyrgyzstan for subsequent delivery to Russia includes the following steps: certification of goods, declaration of goods, payment of customs duties and taxes, customs control, registration of the vehicle, delivery to Russia.
Next, you need to study the documents required to import a car from Kyrgyzstan to Russia.

To begin with, it should be noted that Kyrgyzstan and Russia are both part of the Eurasian Economic Union, so the countries have a common customs territory, which allows trade between the states without customs clearance and without paying customs duties. Thus, a citizen of the Russian Federation importing a car from Kyrgyzstan to Russia will not have to pay customs duties and declare the car when crossing the border, but there are a number of conditions without which it will not be possible to import a vehicle. When importing a car by an individual, it is enough to obtain the SSBKTS and on its basis issue the EPTS, and then, after paying the recycling fee, register the car in the traffic police and install Russian license plates on it.

When importing by legal entities, according to the established rules, you must pay the recycling fee, as well as the costs associated with the registration of imported cars EPTS (electronic passport vehicle), drawn on the basis of SBKTS or single OTTS. When a vehicle imported from Kyrgyzstan is placed on the company's balance sheet, the VAT paid on purchase can be deducted, and for vehicles intended for sale, VAT can be included in the sales value. The amount of recycling fees for legal entities and individual entrepreneurs (PE) depends on the age of the car and engine volume.

All costs during the clearance of the imported car can be called the concept of "customs clearance" and its price can be calculated by the following formula: Customs clearance = payment of the scrap tax + Registration of SCTPS + Registration of EPTS + Duty for license plates.

Also, you should not forget the cost of travel to Kyrgyzstan and ferrying the car back to Russia. Despite all these costs and quite a long road, sometimes buying a car in Kyrgyzstan will be much more profitable than buying it in Russia, so this direction of trade is promising.

In addition to the above-mentioned expenses, the Russian importer will also have to pay VAT. The procedure for payment of VAT on import of goods from the EEU member states is established by Annex No. 18 to the Treaty on the Eurasian Economic Union of May 29, 2014. According to this Annex, when importing goods, VAT is always paid by the owner, to whom the ownership of these goods is transferred, and VAT is paid not to the customs authorities, but to the tax authorities.

The tax base for the calculation of the EAEU VAT includes the value of the goods themselves as indicated in the accompanying documents, as well as the excise tax (if the goods are excisable). If it is necessary to recalculate the value of the goods in Russian rubles, the conversion rate is taken as of the date of acceptance of goods for accounting.

According to item 17 of the Annex the sums of indirect taxes payable on goods imported on the territory of one member state from the territory of another member state shall be calculated by the taxpayer at the tax rates established by the legislation of the member state to the territory of which the goods were imported.

sub_elements.title: 3.4 Logistics and transportation of cars from UAE to Russia
text: Delivery of cars from UAE to Russia can be carried out in several ways: by land transport, sea transport and air transport. Land transport means transportation by freight train or car carrier. Sea transport is transport by ship; the car is loaded into a separate container. Air transport is the fastest and suits customers with tight deadlines, as the car is delivered by cargo plane. For more profitable transportation, a company needs to assemble several cars rather than sending each one individually.

A combination of shipping methods is necessary for quality and budget shipping. Advantages of the combined mode of transportation: efficient use of resources by optimizing the use of vehicles, which will reduce costs; flexibility and scalability, depending on customer requirements and delivery time can be selected the best combination; budget transportation, delivery quality remains at a premium, but in terms of costs, it is more profitable. For example, ground transport is much more profitable than air transport.

Certainly, one of the safest ways is air transport. However, it is also the most expensive. Risks of land transport: road conditions, possibility of an accident. Risks of sea transport: damage to the container and the car. For example, it is very important that the car enters the ship on its own, without the help of a crane. This also reduces the risks of damage to the vehicle. These risks can be avoided by quality and detailed formulation of the route, the study of the roads through which will be the transportation of goods, as well as possible weather conditions.

sub_elements.title: 3.6 Risks and recommendations
text: The study of risks will help to eliminate possible problems arising at the stages of the company. Also their study will help to make balanced decisions in a critical situation. Risks that may arise for Aurora on this project:
Not clarity of the goal set from the client

Pay special attention to understanding customer needs and preferences. Establish mechanisms for defining clear customer goals and requirements to avoid possible delays and inconsistencies in the vehicle sourcing and export process.

Take into account all his detailed wishes about the brand, specifications and equipment of the car. Pay special attention to understanding customer preferences, develop a mechanism for determining customer desires, to help avoid possible difficulties and delays in the vehicle sourcing and export stages.
The risk of low demand

Since this direction appeared relatively recently, there is a risk not to attract enough customers to cover the costs of organizing transportation. Not many people think about the possibility of buying cars from the UAE. People are not sufficiently aware of the possibility of buying a car from other countries and reach for the proven way of buying through dealerships.

That is why the company needs to follow a well-thought-out marketing plan to attract customers and prove to them that buying cars from the UAE can cost them much more than buying a car in the Russian Federation.
The risk of not taking the lead in the UAE car export market

UAE car exporting companies are already present in the market, but according to the above analysis, the company has every chance to become one of the leaders in this field, but it is important to be competitive with your advantages. It is necessary to adhere to high standards at all stages of work. This will give an opportunity to strengthen the company's reputation in the field of exporting cars from the UAE.
Changing external factors

The current situation in the world is unstable, so any activity associated with international trade has certain risks. In particular, it is necessary to monitor all changes in legislation related to import and export of goods. If this is not done in time and do not take measures to change the work plan of the company, you can incur significant financial losses.
Financial risks

It is necessary to carefully search for a car in the UAE market to find the best and most suitable offer. The search for the most favorable offers directly depends on the number of customers the company has, since the lower the cost of the car, the lower will be its final cost and the greater will be the savings to the client compared to the purchase of the car in Russia.
Transportation risks

The above route includes the use of different modes of transportation and as a consequence, several transshipments of cars, which are the responsibility of hired intermediaries. All this carries the risk of damage to the cargo.
Thus, the successful operation of such a business requires careful planning, effective marketing, and regular monitoring of changes in external factors.

table_of_content.title: Conclusion
text: This work was done as a consulting project to develop an export automotive strategy for the company Aurora. After the analysis of the Russian market, we can say that the topic of this project is relevant because the car market in Russia is now in crisis. Many car dealers have left the market because of the world situation. There are no familiar brands on the market or they are being sold at very high prices. UAE automobile market was chosen as an export market for this consulting project. The UAE automotive market is now under development, many car companies are paying a lot of attention to attracting foreign buyers.
The purpose of this work was to develop an Automobile export strategy for Aurora.
In order to fulfill all the goals and objectives stated in this work, the project was divided into 3 parts.
In the first part the analysis of the company was made, as well as the explanation of the management problem.

In the second part, we analyzed the car market in Russia, from which it follows that the market is in crisis because of the increase in car prices and the lack of foreign dealers. The Russian government, in order to solve this problem, emphasizes the development of the Russian car industry, gives citizens the opportunity to buy a domestic car with great benefits. But not all citizens are interested in buying Russian cars and prefer foreign brands, because of that there is a lack of imported cars on the market. Since the company has submitted a request to develop an export strategy for cars from the UAE market, an analysis of the car market in the UAE was made, which showed that the car market is now actively developing, it presents a diverse selection of cars from different manufacturers. Thus, it is able to meet the needs of everyone, regardless of their preferences. Then there was a comparative analysis of prices for cars in the UAE and Russia to make sure that the same cars cost differently in the UAE and Russian market, namely in the UAE car market the same cars cost several times cheaper. After that was made an analysis of potential buyers through a survey which showed that most of all interested in buying cars from the UAE people who are going to use the car for personal purposes. Also these services were of interest to companies that work with cars, such as cabs. Next, an analysis of competitors was made with the help of a competitive profile matrix, which revealed the key players in the market and the main competitors of the company. With the help of this matrix, strengths and aspects that Aurora should work on to take a leading position in the market were identified.

In the third part, we developed an export vehicle strategy for Aurora. First, a marketing strategy was developed, with the help of which we made up the prices and figured out the sales channels that the company would use. Next, we analyzed the nuances of customs clearance and created a logistics route that the company could take as a basis for transporting goods. Then a financial plan was drawn up for the purchase of one vehicle, which showed the monetary prospect of working with this market. The last subchapter of the third part is the risk analysis and development of recommendations on these risks, after which we found out that there are risks in this area, but they can be avoided with good communication with the client, with monitoring of the regulatory framework and timely response to emerging difficulties.

In general, this project provides complete information about the scheme of exporting cars from the UAE and their subsequent import into the Russian Federation from Kyrgyzstan by Aurora. The business of exporting cars from the UAE offers new opportunities and requires careful planning, effective marketing, attention to changing external factors and a risk management strategy to ensure success in this competitive market. Understanding and dealing with the risks possible in this market should give the company a competitive edge. This project may form the basis of the company's further development plan and its subsequent entry into the export market of other countries.

The company is engaged in parallel imports into Russia.  Its main activity consists in individual search of goods around the world in accordance with the requests of clients. That is, the company imports any goods, from clothing to car parts. The main goal of the company is to meet customers' needs for high quality products and fast delivery. The company did not have its own product, as it acted as a dealer in the Russian market. The company wants to make serious changes in its activities, so it has made a request to develop a strategy for buying cars in UAE and then importing them into Russia. Solving this case is a very hot topic at the moment, because of the world situation the automotive market is very depressed. The automotive industry   in Russia  is the most important sector for the economic growth and development of any country. This industry is growing and developing rapidly in different parts of the world, and Russia is one of the largest markets for automotive products. However, due to recent supply chain disruptions and component shortages, many companies in the automotive market are facing problems. For example, many models of cars are in short supply or not available at all in car dealerships, and problems have arisen with the guarantee on cars due to the withdrawal of official dealers. The UAE is one of the largest automotive markets in the Gulf region. The UAE automotive market is represented by many global manufacturers such as Toyota, Nissan, Ford, Chevrolet, BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Audi, and many others. Most of the cars are sold under the "import-sales" scheme, which means that they have been brought into the country from other countries of the world, mainly from Europe, USA and Japan. The condition of cars in the UAE is considered good because in most cases they are in excellent condition and have been thoroughly inspected before being sold. This is because there are strict rules and regulations governing the technical condition  of cars in the UAE, and a thorough inspection is carried out on site before a permit for sale is issued. Car prices in the UAE are lower than in other countries due to the lack of taxes on cars and more affordable fuel prices. Another important element of the UAE car market is car auctions. There are several major car auctions in the UAE where you can buy used cars at a lower price than in the regular market.   At car auctions you can find a wide range of cars of different  brands  and models as well as different classes and categories.  The purpose of this consulting project is to develop a  Automobile export strategy for Aurora . Relevance of the work: The relevance of this project due to the current situation in the automobile market in Russia, which was severely affected by failures in the supply chain and the shortage of components. This has led to a shortage of both new and used vehicles, creating a potential opportunity for new market entrants. Therefore, this paper will develop a strategy for Aurora, a Kyrgyz company, to purchase cars in  UAE  and then  import them into Russia. During the meeting with the company representatives the following aspects were discussed: what cars are the easiest to buy in  UAE ? How to pay for the cars? How to transport the cars and how to bring them to Russia? What about taxes?  All of these aspects will be reflected in this project. Objective of the research: The main objective of this consulting project is to develop a strategy for the purchase of cars in UAE by the Kyrgyz company Aurora with subsequent importation into Russia. To achieve the goal of this work, I will solve the following tasks: Evaluate Aurora's resources and  competencies   Analyze the Russian automotive market Analyze the car market in the UAE To conduct a comparative analysis of prices for cars in Russia and the UAE Analyze the segment of potential buyers  in Russia Analyze the  competitors  in the UAE car market  Analyze possible market strategy  Formation of prices for cars taking into account the competitive environment Examine customs rules and procedures for importing cars from  UAE to  Kyrgyzstan  Examine customs rules and procedures for importing cars from Kyrgyzstan to Russia Make a logistical scheme for delivering cars to Russia Make a financial planning  Analyze the risks   and prepare recommendations  Subject of the study: development of a strategy for the purchase of cars in the UAE by the Kyrgyz company "Aurora" with subsequent importation into Russia. Object of the study: the market of cars in the UAE, the Russian market, customs regulations and procedures for importing cars from Kyrgyzstan to Russia, the segment of potential buyers, the competitive environment, the logistics scheme of car delivery, car prices and risk analysis. Structure of the work: the thesis consists of an introduction,  three  chapters, a conclusion ,  a reference  and an appendix. The first chapter is  about Aurora's analysis and clarification of the manage rial  problem.  The  second  chapter is devoted to the analysis of the UAE and Russian market, as well as aspects of   potential customers and competitors .  The  third  chapter is to develop a strategy for the purchase of cars in the UAE market of the Kyrgyz company Aurora with the subsequent importation of cars into Russia. Research methodology for developing a strategy for the purchase of cars in the UAE by a Kyrgyz company with subsequent importation into Russia: Research Design: The research design will be exploratory and qualitative, aimed at identifying the key factors for the development of a successful strategy for the purchase of cars in the UAE by a Kyrgyz company with subsequent importation into Russia. The study will include a review of existing literature on the Russian automobile market and the UAE market, as well as a review of literature related to logistics and customs activities.  Data collection: The data collection process will involve primary and secondary data sources. Primary data sources will include interviews with Kyrgyz company representatives and experts in the Russian automobile market and the UAE market. Secondary data sources will include industry reports, academic journals and online databases. Internet research: various Internet sources will be used to obtain data on the UAE car market and the Russian car market, to compare prices and delivery conditions for cars, to make logistical charts, to study customs import regulations, and to obtain information on potential competitors and buyers. Questionnaire survey: will be used to collect data on the current situation in the Russian automotive market, preferences and needs of potential buyers to determine the target audience. PESTLE analysis: PESTLE analysis will be conducted to assess political, economic, socio-cultural, technological, legal and environmental factors that may affect the importation of cars into Russia from Kyrgyzstan. Data analysis: various data analysis tools, such as Excel, will be used to analyze data from different sources more accurately and comprehensively. Competitive profile matrix   will be used to compare our company with our competitors. This tool will help assess Aurora's competitiveness in the automotive industry market by comparing its products, quality, prices and other characteristics with its competitors. Market Entry Strategy: Based on the results of PESTLE and other analyses, a strategy will be developed for the purchase of cars in the UAE by a Kyrgyz company with subsequent importation into Russia. The strategy will include recommendations on product range, logistics schemes, customs aspects, pricing. The market entry strategy will also take into account the resources and capabilities of the company and risks. Conclusion:  The results of the study and recommendations will be summarized in the format of a report with a finalized strategy for the purchase of cars in the UAE by a Kyrgyz  company with subsequent importation into Russia. Thus, the conclusion of the project will be based on the generalization of the results of the analysis of the Russian car market and the market of cars in the UAE, as well as a comparative analysis of prices in Russia and the UAE. Also on the results of the analysis of the segment of potential buyers    and competitors. In addition, the conditions of purchase and delivery of cars in Kyrgyzstan, customs regulations and procedures for importing cars from Kyrgyzstan to Russia, and the logistics scheme for delivering cars to Russia will be taken into account. Competitive profile matrix  and PESTEL will help to assess the competitive environment and identify advantages and disadvantages of competitors. Risk analysis and recommendations on pricing for cars will also be carried out,  considering  the competitive environment. The use of the Delphi method will allow to get expert opinion from different stakeholders and take into account their views in the formation of recommendations for the strategy of purchasing cars in the UAE by the Kyrgyz company "Aurora" and then import them into Russia. PART 1.  Company background and the managerial problem  statement 1.1  Company description   Aurora company was founded in 2021 in Bishkek, Kyrgyzstan. The form of a legal entity is a limited liability company. The headquarters is located in Bishkek. Aurora is a trading company, its main activity is the turnkey delivery of products. Aurora works with customers in the Russian Federation. It has its own representatives in Russia, who, if necessary, contact the customer. The company has gained its customer base through, first, "telephone radio", as well as through social networks.  The company searches for, then delivers products from abroad to customers in Russia. Aurora was established as a trading company that imports products to Russia under the customer's order. However, due to the lack of orientation in a certain direction, it is difficult for the company to manage and distribute its resources competently and efficiently. As mentioned above, the company is engaged in the import of absolutely different goods to Russia from auto parts to clothing.  After an interview with representatives of the company, the following task was set, to develop an export strategy for the supply of cars from the UAE to Russia. Due to some events around the world many car dealers left Russia and the car market is in a critical condition. There is a shortage of imported cars in the Russian market. Representatives of the company chose the UAE market for the following reasons: firstly, the company has a representative in the UAE, which would help to resolve any issues related to the transportation of cars from UAE to Kyrgyzstan in a more expeditious and efficient manner; secondly, the company considers this market more suitable for the transportation of cars because before they come to Russia they need to be cleared  in Kyrgyzstan; thirdly, in their opinion prices of cars in UAE differ several times from the prices of cars in Russia.  However, they are not sure about the prospects of working with export cars. That is why it is necessary to study the Russian car market and the UAE car market. It is important to find out how much the cars sold in the UAE are similar to the demands of car buyers from Russia. It is important to study the prices of cars in the UAE and Russia in order to calculate the benefits of working in this direction. Also, to develop in this direction it is necessary to study potential customers, so you can build an effective marketing strategy. As well as to study competitors in order to identify the advantages of the company, as well as the factors on which the company needs to work to improve its competitiveness. In order to understand the profitability of working with export cars from the UAE, it is necessary to calculate the associated costs of logistics and customs. Since the price difference for cars from UAE to Russia may not be so significant after calculating these points.   Thus, the work should begin with an analysis of the automotive market in Russia. Part   2 .  Analysis of the  UAE  and Russian automotive market   2 . 1   Russian Automotive Market Analysis   To begin with, we should consider the concept of the automobile market itself. The automobile market is commonly understood as a set of economic relations that promote interaction between market participants for the exchange of automobile equipment for cash or cash equivalents. In addition, the automobile market is a territorially bounded space used by individual entrepreneurs or individuals for the sale of vehicles and their spare parts.  There are four main segments of the Russian automotive market: passenger cars and light commercial vehicles, trucks, buses and the segment of special purpose vehicles. This project will consider the first segment: passenger cars and light commercial vehicles. To begin with, let's look at the size of the automotive market over the past few years, relying on the following tool: sales statistics from the AEB (Association of European Businesses), which is compiled from data on shipments to dealers provided by the brands themselves and the statistics published by the Federal Customs Service of the Russian Federatio n. The passenger car segment is the most mass market for cars in Russia. Environmental safety laws and policies Figure  3  PESTEL analysis of automobile market of Russia Political factors Political factors 1.  Taxes and import laws In 2022, it became impossible to supply cars to Russia from unfriendly countries. In response to the sanctions restrictions, Russia developed a procedure for parallel imports.   In December 2022, the European Commission published a list of measures against violators of the sanctions regime against Russia. The document provides for liability, including for the supply of sanctioned goods to Russia, up to criminal liability: up to five years of imprisonment. What is at stake here is primarily investment in the Russian economy. In Russia, on the contrary, a number of restrictions have been abolished in response to these measures. Thus, on the contrary, the criminal responsibility for the unauthorized importation of goods by the right holder was abolished in the summer, which opened the possibility for the so-called parallel imports. The introduction of sanctions first turned out to be to the advantage of car sellers in Russia. In the spring of 2022, consumers reacted to the alarming news by hastily withdrawing money from deposits and spending it on something tangible, particularly cars. With panic demand and the cessation of deliveries through official channels, a "seller's market" emerged, where scarce cars began to be sold at incredible markups. Almost immediately there was a demand for alternative supplies of cars.  The main document in the import of cars is the RF Government Decree of May 12, 2022 № 855 "On approval of the rules of mandatory requirements for certain wheeled vehicles and their conformity assessment" (as amended and supplemented), which approves the rules for the application of mandatory requirements in respect of cars. Legal factors  When buying cars, consumers should pay attention to the following points: the seller is obliged in a timely manner in a clear and accessible form to bring to the buyer's attention the necessary and reliable information about the cars.  Including the company name (name) and location (legal address) of the goods manufacturer, location of the organization (organizations), authorized by the manufacturer (seller) to accept claims from buyers and repair and maintenance of goods. If the goods purchased by the buyer were used, or a defect (deficiencies) were eliminated in it, the buyer must be provided with information about it. The seller must warn the buyer about the defects of the goods not only verbally, but also in writing (on the label of goods, sales receipt or in any other way). When selling cars, the seller is obliged to acquaint the consumer at his request with one of the following documents: certificate or declaration of conformity;   a copy of the certificate, certified by the original holder of the certificate, a notary or the body for the certification of goods, which issued the certificate; shipping documents issued by the manufacturer or supplier (seller) and containing for each item of goods information about confirming its compliance with established requirements (certificate number, duration, the authority that issued the certificate or the registration number of the declaration of conformity, its duration, the name of the manufacturer or supplier (seller) that accepted the certificate of conformity) These documents must be signed and sealed by the manufacturer (supplier, seller) with his address and telephone number.   The vehicle warranty is the manufacturer's obligation to repair the vehicle within a specified period of time. The warranty is not provided by an authorized dealer, but by the manufacturer. Therefore, the dealer cannot officially remove the vehicle from the  warranty. He can only refuse warranty repair in a specific case. For this purpose, the dealer checks the quality of the car. The car warranty is given not only by the manufacturer, but also by the law. If there is no warranty period, the buyer has the right to make a claim within two years of purchase. As for the subsidies aimed at supporting the automobile industry in the Russian Federation, they can lead to an increase in the number of hybrid domestic cars. After the company Aurora has received payment for the car, there is a payment for this car in the UAE.  After the purchase of a car from the UAE, you need to collect the following documents for its transportation to Kyrgyzstan.  When taking the car from the UAE to Kyrgyzstan you will need to collect the following documents: passport of the car, technical passport, contract of sale, insurance policy, customs clearance documents. Car Passport This is an official document that certifies ownership and contains information about the car. Information that must be included in this passport: make, model, year of manufacture, vin number and so on.  Technical passport This document contains information on technical characteristics of the vehicle, such as weight, dimensions, engine, etc. Contract of Sale  This document must specify the date of purchase, value. Car Insurance Policy  This is a necessary document that insures your car against possible damage during its transportation. The insurance policy must be valid for the entire period of transportation. As mentioned above, the cost of Aurora directly depends on the value of this insurance policy.  Customs clearance documents This is a declaration of goods, customs invoice. It is important to consider the rules of each country through which the cargo is transported. All of this set of documents is required in order to transport a car from the UAE through the countries to Kyrgyzstan. 3 .3 Required documents for importing and exporting cars from Kyrgyzstan Customs clearance of a vehicle from the UAE upon arrival in Kyrgyzstan for subsequent delivery to Russia includes the following steps: certification of goods , declaration of goods, payment of customs duties and taxes, customs control, registration of the vehicle, delivery to Russia. Next, you need to study the documents required to import a car from Kyrgyzstan to Russia. To begin with, it should be noted that Kyrgyzstan and Russia are both part of the Eurasian Economic Union, so the countries have a common customs territory, which allows trade between the states without customs clearance and without paying customs duties. Thus, a citizen of the Russian Federation importing a car from Kyrgyzstan to Russia will not have to pay customs duties and declare the car when crossing the border, but there are a number of conditions without which it will not be possible to import a vehicle. When importing a car by an individual, it is enough to obtain the SSBKTS and on its basis issue the EPTS, and then, after paying the recycling fee, register the car in the traffic police and install Russian license plates on it.  When importing by legal entities, according to the established rules, you must pay the recycling fee, as well as the costs associated with the registration of imported cars EPTS (electronic passport vehicle), drawn on the basis of SBKTS or single OTTS. When a vehicle imported from Kyrgyzstan is placed on the company's balance sheet, the VAT paid on purchase can be deducted, and for vehicles intended for sale, VAT can be included in the sales value. The amount of recycling fees for legal entities and individual entrepreneurs (PE) depends on the age of the car and engine volume.  All costs during the clearance of the imported car can be called the concept of "customs clearance" and its price can be calculated by the following formula: Customs clearance = payment of the scrap tax + Registration of SCTPS + Registration of EPTS + Duty for license plates.  Also, you should not forget the cost of travel to Kyrgyzstan and ferrying the car back to Russia. Despite all these costs and quite a long road, sometimes buying a car in Kyrgyzstan will be much more profitable than buying it in Russia, so this direction of trade is promising. In addition to the above-mentioned expenses, the Russian importer will also have to pay VAT. The procedure for payment of VAT on import of goods from the EEU member states is established by Annex No. 18 to the Treaty on the Eurasian Economic Union of May 29, 2014. According to this Annex, when importing goods, VAT is always paid by the owner, to whom the ownership of these goods is transferred, and VAT is paid not to the customs authorities, but to the tax authorities.  The tax base for the calculation of the EAEU VAT includes the value of the goods themselves as indicated in the accompanying documents, as well as the excise tax (if the goods are excisable). If it is necessary to recalculate the value of the goods in Russian rubles, the conversion rate is taken as of the date of acceptance of goods for accounting.  According to item 17 of the Annex the sums of indirect taxes payable on goods imported on the territory of one member state from the territory of another member state shall be calculated by the taxpayer at the tax rates established by the legislation of the member state to the territory of which the goods were imported.  That is, when importing goods into the territory of the Russian Federation from the territory of the EEU countries, importers (buyers) pay VAT at the rates established by the tax legislation of the Russian Federation depending on the type of goods (20%, 10%).  As a general rule, the rate of 20% is applied.  The 10% rate applies for food, children's, medical and other socially important goods specified in clause 2, article 164 of the Tax Code.  Thus, when importing cars from Kyrgyzstan into the Russian Federation the VAT is paid by the importer (the buyer) with the VAT rate of 20%. 3 . 4   Logistics and transportation of cars from UAE to Russia Delivery of cars from UAE to Russia can be carried out in several ways: by land transport, sea transport and air transport. Land transport means transportation by freight train or car carrier. Sea transport is transport by ship; the car is loaded into a separate container. Air transport is the fastest and suits customers with tight deadlines, as the car is delivered by cargo plane. For more profitable transportation, a company needs to assemble several cars rather than sending each one individually.  A combination of shipping methods is necessary for quality and budget shipping. Advantages of the combined mode of transportation: efficient use of resources by optimizing the use of vehicles, which will reduce costs; flexibility and scalability, depending on customer  requirements and delivery time can be selected the best combination; budget transportation, delivery quality remains at a premium, but in terms of costs, it is more profitable. For example, ground transport is much more profitable than air transport.   Certainly, one of the safest ways is air transport. However, it is also the most expensive. Risks of land transport: road conditions, possibility of an accident. Risks of sea transport: damage to the container and the car. For example, it is very important that the car enters the ship on its own, without the help of a crane. This also reduces the risks of damage to the vehicle. These risks can be avoided by quality and detailed formulation of the route, the study of the roads through which will be the transportation of goods, as well as possible weather conditions. Thus, the successful operation of such a business requires careful planning, effective marketing, and regular monitoring of changes in external factors. 1. Оценка влияния экономических санкций на автомобильный рынок России.
2. Анализ перспектив развития транспортно-логистического сервиса на территории ЕАЭС.

Задачи:
1. Изучение статистики продаж легковых автомобилей и коммерческих транспортных средств на территории России за период 2018-2023.
2. Определение основных факторов, влияющих на стоимость автомобилей в России, включая стоимость нефти и курс доллара США.
3. Анализ регуляторных мер, направленных на поддержку автомобильной промышленности в России, включая государственную программу покупки автомобиля в 2023 году.
4. Исследование влияния политических и экономических факторов на автомобильную индустрию в 2022 году.
5. Оценка интереса потребителей к покупке автомобилей из ОАЭ и определение ключевых факторов, влияющих на выбор поставщика автомобилей из этой страны.
6. Анализ возможностей и проблем, связанных с таможенным оформлением экспорта автомобилей в ОАЭ.
7. Изучение опыта компаний, работающих в сфере параллельного импорта автомобилей в России, включая анализ логистических аспектов этого процесса.
8. Оценка влияния экономических санкций на транспортные логику и перевозки в Российской Федерации.
